Norman La Force
802 Balra Drive
Cerrito. CA 94530-3002
Mr. scort Ha[abrin. Esq. 
May 14' 1992
Ceneral Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
At the suggestion of Mr. John Wallace, an attorney in your
office, I am writing to you to confirm the a-dvice that he gave me
over the telephone on May 12, 
"992. 
This advice concerns a question
I had about a possible conflict of interest.
FACTS
I am an attomey employed by the CIGNA insurance companies
in CIGNA's San Francisco Field Litigation office. CIGNA assigns to us
cases in which we defend CIGNA insureds in tort claims brought
against those insureds. Recently, I as assigned a case in which
CIGNA's insured, Security Owners Corporation, was sued. This case
arises out of a claim of personal injury by a plumber who was
working at a subdivision at which Security Owners was the owner
and general contractor.
I receive no compensation of any kind liom Security Owners
for this representation. I am a salaried employee of CIGNA. Since
the case occi.irred two years ago Security Owners mal oi may not be
a current insured of CIGNA.
I am also a member of the City Council of El Cerrito.
Currently, Security Owners has before the City an application for the
construction of a small subdivision in the city. This is a land use
and zoning matter with no connection in any way to the personal
injr-rr1, 61oi. of the plumber in Napa.
EI


IELEPHONE LEGAL OPIN ION
I informed Mr. Wallace of the above facts and asked him if
there was any kind of conflict of interest under FPPC laws and
regulations. He stated to me that there was none. He gave the basis
of his opinion as follows. First, I receive no compensation from
Security Owners and the fact that the company may be a client of my
employer does not create a conflict since I receive no compensation
from Security Owners. Second, there is no nexus between the matter
in El Cerrito involving a land use decision and the defense of a
personal injury action arising out of alleged acts or omissions in
Napa, California.
If I have misstated my conversation with Mr. Wallace,
Commission believes that this advice was incorrect, please
know as soon as possible.
Sincerely yours,

sections.question_synthetic: Does an attorney employed by an insurance company have a conflict of interest under FPPC laws when representing an insured in a personal injury case while also serving on a city council that is reviewing a land use application from the same insured?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a member of the public seeking to confirm telephone advice, not an FPPC response letter. The document references a telephone legal opinion but does not contain the FPPC's formal written response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in the document itself. The extraction confidence is moderate due to document quality/formatting issues and the fact that this appears to be the requestor's letter rather than the FPPC's formal advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Does an attorney employed by an insurance company have a conflict of interest under FPPC laws when representing an insured in a personal injury case while also serving on a city council that is reviewing a land use application from the same insured?

embedding.summary: Norman La Force, an attorney employed by CIGNA and a member of the El Cerrito City Council, seeks confirmation of telephone advice received from FPPC attorney John Wallace regarding whether his representation of a CIGNA insured in a personal injury case creates a conflict of interest with his city council duties, given that the insured has a pending land use application before the city.
